Currently have Moon Camp settled on the northernmost shore of Janthir, not far from the ash lands. Kryptis everywhere. At least we're far enough away from the main populations to not worry much about civilian casualties. Supposed to be some kodan nearby, but I think they're currently living in their southern settlement. Given what intel we do have on them, I suspect they're not going to need much help from us either way. Anything that chooses to live in Janthir is probably tough as Deldrimor steel.    The Kryptis are swarming at the shoreline, pointed toward the isles. They never cross over, though. Not sure if that's by choice. Not sure I want to know, either. Mabon's got the isles covered, and I've got plenty to kill.    —Camp Leader Wihlford

Dad, Mom passed along your request. I've pulled some materials on Janthir, mursaat, and the titans from the archives and left them in your satchel with this letter. Please make sure they make it back to the Priory; Magister Stonehealer will have my head otherwise. Wish I could've gone with you, but my manipulations are rusty, and I'd just get in your way. That and my archival duties have me tied up, too. Sestina and I have been cataloguing and organizing all of the new reports and research coming in from the Janthir expeditions for printing and distribution among the alliance. Sorry, I'm rambling now. Please, just come back in one piece. Best of luck, Kurt PS. If you DO kill a titan, I wouldn't mind a fresh sample of its exo-shell, or one of its organs if you can sneak it out. Might be safer to do it aboveboard, though, if the smuggling rumors are true. The Priory would appreciate it!
Dadga needs to speak with you in the Wizard's Tower.

Dagda—    Zojja has ties to the wayfinder—much as she might not remember them—and she's determined to volunteer. Makes sense. But I think you're making a mistake joining her.    Don't get me wrong; we'd all rather see Peitha on the throne than Eparch. Just not sure anything short of Isgarren facing Eparch himself is going to help. Not to mention that you've barely recovered from the ritual that knocked that Nourys creature out of Amnytas. I saw how much that took out of you. Are you sure you're up to this? I know I'm not going to stop you if you're set on it, but, Dagda, if this fails, and you're not here... Amnytas, the tower, and Tyria are all going to be vulnerable. We need you.    If you've already left, well... Guess I'll throw this in the hearth next time I build a fire.    —Lyhr

Dalnim came back today. I made soup—she loves my soup. My face must've been red as a plum.There's something about her that I can't quite place... She's rough. I've seen her around town before, and I don't want to jump to conclusions, but I know the Am Fah when I see them. Their confidence, the scars. They make the Brotherhood look like a bunch of louts. Which isn't wrong, if I had to say.I don't want anything to do with that life. I saw what it did to Papa. I don't recognize him, not anymore. But maybe I can't hide from that life any longer, not with everything else going on. The attacks, the plots... Her.

Dalnim,After reading your field report, I've decided that Yuhwa provides little to no vital use in our intel. I would like you to dispose of her—swiftly. I suspect that the gravity of her death will rattle the Jade Brotherhood more than any information she could provide us.Let me know when the job is done. And make it hurt, just a little.Mina
